nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: 鹃形目
nodes.summary: 鹃形目（Cuculiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联148种鸟类，如圭拉鹃、Greater ani、滑嘴犀鵑等148种鸟类。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: Sumatran ground cuckoo
nodes.summary: Sumatran ground cuckoo（Sumatran ground cuckoo，学名 Carpococcyx viridis），分类上属于鹃形目，并归入杜鹃科，在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，常见栖息地包括山地森林，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、森林砍伐、小种群风险。

nodes.name: 山地森林
nodes.summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

nodes.name: 杜鹃科
nodes.summary: 杜鹃科（Cuculidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联147种鸟类，如圭拉鹃、Greater ani、滑嘴犀鵑等147种鸟类。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The Sumatran ground cuckoo is endemic to the Indonesian island of Sumatra. Notes on early specimen labels as well as recent sightings suggest its favoured habitat is foothills and primary montane forest. It has been found between elevations of , although recent sightings are exclusively from .{{Cite book |last1=Payne |first1=Robert B

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Due to ongoing habitat loss and small population size, the IUCN Red List evaluates the Sumatran ground cuckoo as endangered. The Red List estimates that the total population of Sumatran ground cuckoos ranges from 50 to 249 mature individuals, and its population is thought to be decreasing. The bird's known range overlaps with some of the Barisan Mountains' 20 protected areas, but rapid deforestation – its main threat – is prevalent even in these areas. A 2003 study showed an average 2% loss of forest within Bukit Barisan Selatan National Park, part of its known range, between 1985 and 1999. This deforestation is caused predominantly by logging, palm oil plantations, pulpwood plantations, establishment of farms, and wildfires. The species likely benefits from protected areas, but its conservation needs are not fully understood due to a lack of study
